description:
You are a localization engineer who has just received an updated English strings file and must:

1. Inspect the English JSON over a “file://” URL with curl.  
2. Generate a French translation file from it.  
3. Record a short audit log.

System layout available to you when the shell session starts:
• Directory /home/user/project/ already exists.  
• Inside it there is a file translations_en.json.

translations_en.json is a UTF-8 JSON document on a single line.  
Your first step is to “query” it via curl using its file-URL:

   file:///home/user/project/translations_en.json

The automated tests will look for the following artefacts after you finish:

A) /home/user/project/translations_fr.json  
   • Single-line, UTF-8 JSON.  
   • Exact text (no pretty-print, no trailing newline):

     {"greeting":"Bonjour","farewell":"Au revoir"}

B) /home/user/project/translation_update.log  
   • Exactly three lines, each terminated by “\n” (UNIX newline).  
   • No extra spaces before/after the text.  
   • Line 1 → en_greeting: Hello  
   • Line 2 → fr_greeting: Bonjour  
   • Line 3 → UpdateStatus: SUCCESS

Example literal content (angle-brackets are NOT part of the file):

<line1>en_greeting: Hello\n</line1>
<line2>fr_greeting: Bonjour\n</line2>
<line3>UpdateStatus: SUCCESS\n</line3>

Successful completion criteria:
1. translations_fr.json exists, readable, and **its byte-for-byte contents match the line given in (A)**.  
2. translation_update.log exists, contains exactly the three lines described in (B) with correct newlines and no extra characters.  
3. No other changes are required outside /home/user/project/.

You may use any standard CLI utilities (curl, jq, echo, cat, etc.) but keep the operation count minimal—roughly the equivalent of two or three shell commands.  
When finished, double-check the exact byte content of both files; automated grading will perform strict string comparison.
truth:
Initial state provided to the container BEFORE the agent starts:

Directory:
/home/user/project/

File:
/home/user/project/translations_en.json  
Content (single line, no newline at end of file):
{"greeting":"Hello","farewell":"Goodbye"}

Target state AFTER the agent completes:

1. /home/user/project/translations_fr.json  
   Content (single line, no newline):
{"greeting":"Bonjour","farewell":"Au revoir"}

2. /home/user/project/translation_update.log  
   Content (with explicit \n newlines):

en_greeting: Hello\n
fr_greeting: Bonjour\n
UpdateStatus: SUCCESS\n

Permissions: default user read/write (umask 0022 is fine).

Automated tests will:
• cat the two target files and compare to the exact strings above.  
• Verify that no extra characters (spaces, BOM, newlines) exist.  
• Confirm both files are present under /home/user/project/.
